column1: # Publishes the species-tree overlay of one sequence-group set: the
# deconvolution union tables (Script 002), the per-species sequence map
# (Script 003), and the composite clades (Script 004). Script 001's standard
# Build with (from the subproject root):  bash RUN-update_upload_to_server.sh
# This is a COPYME template; copy it into the canonical workflow-RUN_* dir to publish.

column6: One row per CURATED composite clade (from INPUT_user/composite_clades_manifest.tsv): identifier, algorithm, definition, and the count of sequence groups that match it. A composite clade is a question about where a group's member species fall on the species tree, evaluated by one of four algorithms: exact (members from EXACTLY the listed components), absent (members absent from ALL listed clades), core_urclade (members in an outgroup of the target AND an ingroup; the target's Ur=last-common-ancestor core), core_early_clade (members in two or more ingroups; the target's Early=early descendant branches). Default building blocks: the five metazoan clades Ctenophora Porifera Placozoa Cnidaria Bilateria within Metazoa, plus NonMetazoa.

Every sequence group with one column per algorithm: its exact composite clade (one per group when curated, else None), and the comma lists of absent / core_urclade / core_early_clade composite clades it matches (independent tests, so a group can match several), else None.

column4: Composite clade detail tables — group identifiers + member sequences per clade
column6: One table per curated composite clade: each row a matching sequence group, with the member SEQUENCE identifiers in each relevant clade.

column1: # ----- Species-tree deconvolution (Script 002, union over all structures) -----

column4: Tree counts — deconvolution (member species per clade)
column6: Species-tree deconvolution: per sequence group, the count of DISTINCT member species within every non-redundant clade across all 105 structures, one column per clade root to tips. A full-coverage (root) clade equals the group's Species_Count.

column4: Tree counts — deconvolution (member sequences per clade)
column6: The companion member-SEQUENCE (protein) counts per clade for each sequence group; same clade columns as the species table. A full-coverage (root) clade equals the group's Sequence_Count.

The wide per-species companion to the deconvolution: per sequence group, one column per species (Genus_species) holding that group's member sequence identifiers in that species. The wide form of the standard membership.

column1: # ----- Standard membership (Script 001) — build internal, NOT published -----

column6: The producer-agnostic membership (SequenceGroup_ID, Sequence_Identifier, Genus_Species) every overlay reads. Build internal; flip to yes to expose.
